key_points: Land revenue was the main source of income for the British government in India
The British introduced different land revenue systems to collect taxes from farmers
The Permanent Settlement was introduced in Bengal by Lord Cornwallis in 1793
Zamindars were responsible for collecting land revenue from peasants
Indigo was a plant used to produce blue dye for cloth
European planters forced peasants to grow indigo instead of food crops
Peasants protested against forced indigo cultivation, leading to the Indigo Revolt
examples: Zamindars collecting taxes from farmers in villages
Peasants growing indigo plants in Bengal
Peasants refusing to grow indigo during the Indigo Revolt
formulas: Permanent Settlement → Fixed land revenue + Zamindars responsible for payment
Indigo cultivation system → Planters give advance → Peasants forced to grow indigo
Peasant protest → Exploitation + Forced farming = Indigo Revolt
exam_tips: Remember the meaning of Permanent Settlement
Know the role of zamindars and peasants
Remember why indigo cultivation was unpopular among peasants
Mention the Indigo Revolt when explaining peasant resistance
